pattern: Each candidate must be a subsequence of the source string; among those that fit, keep the longest, breaking ties by smallest lexicographic order.
hints: A dictionary word is formable iff it is a subsequence of the source string.
Use a two-pointer scan to check subsequence membership for each candidate.
Track the best formable word, preferring greater length and, on ties, lexicographically smaller.

edge_inputs.description: only single-letter words fit, smallest chosen
equal-length formable words, lexicographically smaller wins
no dictionary word is a subsequence
letters present but out of order is not a subsequence
repeated letters needed beyond what source provides

stress.description: long source with random dictionary words sometimes subsequences
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abcde') for _ in range(size))
    dic = []
    for _ in range(40):
        L = rng.randint(1, 8)
        dic.append(''.join(rng.choice('abcde') for _ in range(L)))
    return [s, dic]
